data/text/trainers.inc.Route133_Text_1C2FC7: It's way more fun to do things the way\nI want than to be like everybody else.\lI mean, that's obvious!
data/text/trainers.inc.Route133_Text_1C302D: I came all the way out here with my\nBIRD POKéMON.
data/text/trainers.inc.Route133_Text_1C307E: I'd like to go back to FORTREE, but I've\ngrown to like this place, too.
data/text/trainers.inc.Route134_Text_1C30C6: Even those POKéMON that can swim are\ncarried along by the rapid currents.
data/text/trainers.inc.Route134_Text_1C311A: I think POKéMON enjoy the fast-running\ncurrents around these parts.
data/text/trainers.inc.Route134_Text_1C315E: My LUVDISC are looking for a fun\nmatch. Will you join us?
data/text/trainers.inc.Route134_Text_1C31A0: There's a collector who's after the\nSCALES of LUVDISC.
data/text/trainers.inc.Route134_Text_1C31D7: Okeydokey! That's enough rest, gang!\nIt's time for a match!
data/text/trainers.inc.Route134_Text_1C3229: My BIRD POKéMON get tired quickly after\na long flight...
data/text/trainers.inc.Route134_Text_1C3262: You're a POKéMON TRAINER.\nNo need for words. We battle now.
data/text/trainers.inc.Route134_Text_1C32D6: It was I who challenged you, and yet\nI lost. I am deeply shamed...
data/text/trainers.inc.Route134_Text_1C3319: The savage tide in this area serves to\nmake us stronger than ever.
data/text/trainers.inc.Route134_Text_1C336E: We will return for more training at\nMETEOR FALLS.\pIf you'd like, you should go, too.\nIt will definitely toughen you up!

data/text/tv.inc.FallarborTown_ContestLobby_Text_1A6F7C:
Oh, hello! You were in a POKéMON CONTEST,
weren't you?\lIt's easy to tell from your POKéMON.\pI'm a reporter. I'm working on a story
on POKéMON CONTESTS.\pIf I may, would you be willing to answer
a few questions?
data/text/tv.inc.FallarborTown_ContestLobby_Text_1A704E: Oh, you will?\nThank you.\pBriefly, how would you describe the\nCONTEST you just entered?
data/text/tv.inc.FallarborTown_ContestLobby_Text_1A70A5:
Ah, I see.
That's a very edifying comment.\pYou get a good feel for what the
CONTEST was like.\pI've got one last question.\pWhen you hear the word “{STR_VAR_2},”
what image do you get?
data/text/tv.inc.FallarborTown_ContestLobby_Text_1A7153:
I see!\pSo that's how you imagine the concept
of “{STR_VAR_2}” to be.\pThank you!
You've given me some good ideas.\pI think I can write a good story on
POKéMON CONTESTS now.\pMaybe, just maybe, this story will even
make it to television.\lI hope you'll look forward to it!
data/text/tv.inc.FallarborTown_ContestLobby_Text_1A7256: Oh, too bad...\pWell, if you come across a good story,\nplease do share it with me.
data/text/tv.inc.FallarborTown_ContestLobby_Text_1A72A8: I'll be looking forward to your next\nPOKéMON CONTEST.
data/text/tv.inc.gTVBravoTrainerText1:
Yeah!
It's BRAVO TRAINER time!\pToday, we're going to profile a POKéMON
belonging to {STR_VAR_1}.\pNow, this POKéMON boasts a {STR_VAR_2}
rating in the {STR_VAR_3} Rank.
data/text/tv.inc.gTVBravoTrainerText2: Introducing {STR_VAR_2} the\n{STR_VAR_1}!\pThe nickname {STR_VAR_2}...\pEven the nickname exudes an air that\nproclaims “{STR_VAR_3}”!
data/text/tv.inc.gTVBravoTrainerText3: Anyway, when the TRAINER {STR_VAR_1}\nentered the POKéMON in a CONTEST,\lwe managed to get a few impassioned\lquotes about the trusty partner.
data/text/tv.inc.gTVBravoTrainerText4:
Asked about the CONTEST afterwards,
{STR_VAR_1} happily replied with a huge\lgrin, “{STR_VAR_2}!”\pWell, sure, {STR_VAR_1}'s POKéMON came in
number {STR_VAR_3} in the CONTEST.\pThat line perfectly suits {STR_VAR_1}
right now, I'd say!
data/text/tv.inc.gTVBravoTrainerText5:
Asked about the CONTEST afterwards,
{STR_VAR_1} replied with a tinge of\lbitterness, “{STR_VAR_2}.”\pWell, sure, {STR_VAR_1}'s POKéMON came in
number {STR_VAR_3} in the CONTEST.\p{STR_VAR_1}'s disappointment comes across
loud and clear, I'd say!
data/text/tv.inc.gTVBravoTrainerText6:
Wouldn't you also like to know what
{STR_VAR_1} imagines {STR_VAR_2} to be?\pYou bet we did!
So we asked, of course!\pThe answer is all perfectly condensed:
“{STR_VAR_3}!”\pThat's what the concept of {STR_VAR_2}
represents to {STR_VAR_1}!
data/text/tv.inc.gTVBravoTrainerText7: The last move {STR_VAR_2} used by\nthe {STR_VAR_1} is entirely about\l“{STR_VAR_3}”!
data/text/tv.inc.gTVBravoTrainerText8:
Bravo, {STR_VAR_1}!
Bravo, {STR_VAR_2}!\pI hope we can count on seeing
{STR_VAR_1} scale even greater heights!\pThat's all the time we have!
Until next time, see you!
data/text/tv.inc.BattleTower_Lobby_Text_1A776D:
Hello! You're the TRAINER who just had
a battle, right?\pI'm gathering interviews with TRAINERS
all over the place.\pMay I get a few words from you about
your impressions on battling?
data/text/tv.inc.BattleTower_Lobby_Text_1A7823:
You will? Really?
Thank you!\lThen, uh...\pHow did things turn out in the BATTLE
TOWER today?\pWere you satisfied with the battle?
Or are you unhappy?
data/text/tv.inc.BattleTower_Lobby_Text_1A78B7: Oh...\nSorry we disturbed you.\pPlease give us an interview the next\ntime you visit the BATTLE TOWER.
data/text/tv.inc.BattleTower_Lobby_Text_1A791B: Well, of course!\pThat unmistakable look of satisfaction\non your face...\pIt's obvious that you've had a great\nbattle.
data/text/tv.inc.BattleTower_Lobby_Text_1A7990: Oh, I see...\pWell, it certainly is difficult to make a\nbattle turn out exactly as planned.
data/text/tv.inc.BattleTower_Lobby_Text_1A79EB: Oh, oh, may I ask one more question?\pIf you were to describe your\nimpressions about this battle with one\lsaying, what would it be?
data/text/tv.inc.BattleTower_Lobby_Text_1A7A6E: Oh, that is stunningly cool!\pThat's a great line!\nI hope you'll do great next time, too.\pI hope to see you again!
data/text/tv.inc.BattleTower_Lobby_Text_1A7AE0: Oh, I see...\pStill, being the silent type is also cool,\nisn't it?\pI hope you'll give me the opportunity to\nshare your thoughts again!
data/text/tv.inc.BattleTower_Lobby_Text_1A7B66: I'll be looking forward to your next\nbattle!
data/text/tv.inc.gTVBravoTrainerBattleTowerText1:
Yeah!
It's BRAVO TRAINER time!\pToday, we're going to profile {STR_VAR_1},
who took the BATTLE TOWER challenge!\pFor the challenge, {STR_VAR_1} entered one
wicked {STR_VAR_2}.
data/text/tv.inc.gTVBravoTrainerBattleTowerText2: The pair set a new record of {STR_VAR_2} wins\nin a row in Level {STR_VAR_1} competition!\lBravo, TRAINER!
data/text/tv.inc.gTVBravoTrainerBattleTowerText3:
The twosome finally succumbed to
{STR_VAR_1} in match number {STR_VAR_2}.\lNice try, TRAINER!\pBut, hey, it's just bad luck to run into
{STR_VAR_1} so early in the challenge.\pWe asked the TRAINER for impressions
on the match with {STR_VAR_1}.
data/text/tv.inc.gTVBravoTrainerBattleTowerText4:
The twosome won it all by defeating
{STR_VAR_1}'s {STR_VAR_2} thoroughly.\lBravo, TRAINER!\pKnocking off even {STR_VAR_1}...
It defies belief! Simply astounding!\pWe asked the TRAINER for impressions
on the moment of glory.
data/text/tv.inc.gTVBravoTrainerBattleTowerText5:
After a string of wins, the pair finally
succumbed to {STR_VAR_1}'s {STR_VAR_2},\ltheir final hurdle.\pNice try, TRAINER!\pStill, you have to give credit.
You don't see many famous combinations\llike {STR_VAR_1} and {STR_VAR_2}.\pWe asked the TRAINER for impressions
on battling the celebrity pair.
data/text/tv.inc.gTVBravoTrainerBattleTowerText6:
This is what the TRAINER had to say:
“I'm satisfied!”\pNow isn't that a refreshing reply?
Bravo, TRAINER!\pIsn't it out-and-out awesome to be able
to battle to full satisfaction?\pI found out exactly how satisfied
when I heard the TRAINER say this:
data/text/tv.inc.gTVBravoTrainerBattleTowerText7:
This is what the TRAINER had to say:
“I'm not satisfied...”\pOur TRAINER was obviously a little down
when that was uttered.\pStill, it's not easy to be able to battle
with complete satisfaction, am I right?\pAnyway, I found out how dissatisfied
our TRAINER was when I heard this:
data/text/tv.inc.gTVBravoTrainerBattleTowerText13:
“{STR_VAR_1}.”
Now isn't that great?\pIt really expresses {STR_VAR_2}'s joy,
I'd say.\pThat battle with {STR_VAR_3} at the
end... It really was what you'd call\l“{STR_VAR_1}”!
data/text/tv.inc.gTVBravoTrainerBattleTowerText14:
“{STR_VAR_1}.”
Now isn't that fitting?\pThat battle with {STR_VAR_3} at the
end... You can't describe it as anything\lelse but “{STR_VAR_1}”!\p{STR_VAR_2}'s disappointment comes across
loud and clear, I'd say!
data/text/tv.inc.gTVBravoTrainerBattleTowerText15:
Bravo, {STR_VAR_1}!
Bravo, {STR_VAR_2}!\pI hope we can count on seeing
{STR_VAR_1} scale even greater heights!\pThat's all the time we have!
Until next time, see you!
data/text/tv.inc.SlateportCity_PokemonFanClub_Text_1A82F1:
Wow!\pIt's plain to see that you lavish your
love on your {STR_VAR_1}.\pOkay, it's named {STR_VAR_2}.\pCan I ask you a favor?\pI'm a TV reporter, and I'm running
a survey on POKéMON.\pWould you be willing to answer a few
simple questions for me?
data/text/tv.inc.SlateportCity_PokemonFanClub_Text_1A83D0: Great! Thank you!\pOkay, here goes.\nI just need quick answers, okay?
data/text/tv.inc.SlateportCity_PokemonFanClub_Text_1A8414: When you first met {STR_VAR_1}, what\ndid you feel?\pHow would you describe your feelings\nat the time?
data/text/tv.inc.SlateportCity_PokemonFanClub_Text_1A8470: Your {STR_VAR_1} is cared for lovingly.\pIf you were to liken it to something\nthat you like, what would it be?
data/text/tv.inc.SlateportCity_PokemonFanClub_Text_1A84D5: This question also relates to your\nbeloved {STR_VAR_1}.\pWhat was it about {STR_VAR_1} that\nattracted you?
data/text/tv.inc.SlateportCity_PokemonFanClub_Text_1A852D: Okay, that makes sense.\pThe next question might be a little\non the tough side.\pHere goes...\pWhat do POKéMON mean to you?
data/text/tv.inc.SlateportCity_PokemonFanClub_Text_1A85A6:
I see!\pHmhm...\pOkay!
Thanks for helping me out.\pIt was fun and enlightening chatting
with you.\pIt's possible that our interview will end
up on TV. Tune in and check!\pOkay, that's all.
Bye-bye!
data/text/tv.inc.SlateportCity_PokemonFanClub_Text_1A8667: Oh, okay...\pWell, if you get the urge to tell me\nabout POKéMON, I'll be here!
data/text/tv.inc.SlateportCity_PokemonFanClub_Text_1A86B5: I enjoy this job - you get to learn so\nmuch about POKéMON by doing\linterviews.
data/text/tv.inc.SlateportCity_PokemonFanClub_Text_1A8704:
Hi, you seem to be very close to your
{STR_VAR_1}.\pDo you know what?
I'm a TV reporter.\pI travel around interviewing people
about POKéMON.\pI'm wondering if you'd be willing to tell
me a little abut your {STR_VAR_1}?

and I shout them out on your behalf!\lIsn't it a fantastic program concept?\pToday, we bring you this report from
our reporter, who we sent out to the\lPOKéMON FAN CLUB.\pSo, just who is today's featured
POKéMON fan?\p... ... ... ... ... ... ... ...\p{STR_VAR_1}!\pSo, let's hear what {STR_VAR_1} has to
say about {STR_VAR_3} the {STR_VAR_2}.\pAnd, I will shout those words of love
out loud on TV!\pHoo-hah!\pLet's shout!
data/text/tv.inc.gTVFanClubOpinionsText2: We asked {STR_VAR_1}, “When you first\nlaid eyes on your {STR_VAR_2}, what was\lyour initial thought?“\p“{STR_VAR_3}!“\pYeahah! That's a mighty fine shout!\pDoesn't it bring back memories of those\ndays long gone by?

data/text/tv.inc.gTVFanClubOpinionsText4: And let's see...\nWhat was it about that {STR_VAR_2}\lthat so attracted {STR_VAR_1}?\p... ... ... ... ... ... ... ...\p“{STR_VAR_3}!”\pWhoa! Such a spectacular declaration!\pThe love of this TRAINER for the\n{STR_VAR_2} comes across loud and clear!
data/text/tv.inc.gTVFanClubOpinionsText5:
Hm? Oh, there's still more.
Let's check it out!\pLet me see, now...\pWe asked {STR_VAR_1}, “What do POKéMON
mean to you?”\p... ... ... ... ... ...\p“{STR_VAR_3}!”\pBravo!\pThat's the best shout I've had all day!\p“{STR_VAR_3}!”\pIt makes you want to shout it out loud

time to say good-bye until next time!\pSo, let's all have one last shout!

data/text/tv.inc.gTVRecentHappeningsText1:
Hello, it's time for RECENT HAPPENINGS.\pFor POKéMON TRAINERS, every day is
a storybook tale.\pWhat we want to do is to introduce you
to some of these POKéMON tales.\pToday, we bring you the story of the
TRAINER {STR_VAR_1}.\pWhat did {STR_VAR_1} experience recently?
Let's find out.\pLet's see...
data/text/tv.inc.gTVRecentHappeningsText2: Wasn't that enlightening?\pThe story gives you a clear idea of what\n{STR_VAR_1} has experienced recently.\lIt's as if we were there as witnesses!
data/text/tv.inc.gTVRecentHappeningsText3: “{STR_VAR_3}.” That\naccents the tale and gives it depth.
data/text/tv.inc.gTVRecentHappeningsText4: “{STR_VAR_3}.”\nThat gives the tale a sense of place.\lIt lets us envision the tale's setting.
data/text/tv.inc.gTVRecentHappeningsText5: The “{STR_VAR_3}”\nsection of the tale is very expressive.
data/text/tv.inc.gTVRecentHappeningsText6:
{STR_VAR_1} has recounted a wonderful
tale involving POKéMON.\pAnd now {STR_VAR_1}'s tale is indelibly
etched into your soul, too.\pThat's it for today.
Please tune in next time.
data/text/tv.inc.gTVPokemonOutbreakText:
Greetings!
It's time for POKéMON NEWS.\pWe've just received word of a very
rare occurrence.\pThere have been reports of a mass
outbreak of {STR_VAR_2} in the vicinity\lof {STR_VAR_1}.\p{STR_VAR_2}, as you're probably aware,
is known as a POKéMON that's rare\land hard to find.\pIt sounds like a rare opportunity to
see the mystifying outbreak of\l{STR_VAR_2} in the wild.\pThat's the news on POKéMON NEWS.
data/text/tv.inc.gTVNameRaterText1:
And now, it's time for...
THE NAME RATER SHOW.\pI tell your POKéMON's fortune from
the nickname you've bestowed.\pAdvice is what I have to give, and it is
helpful advice that I offer.\pToday, I shall prophesize the nickname
{STR_VAR_3} of {STR_VAR_1}'s POKéMON\l{STR_VAR_2}.\pHmhm...\pHmm...
This nickname is...
data/text/tv.inc.gTVNameRaterText2: A nickname that hints at talent in many\ndifferent ways.\pI urge this TRAINER to take courage\nand take on many challenges.
data/text/tv.inc.gTVNameRaterText3: A nickname that perfectly complements\n{STR_VAR_1}, the TRAINER's name.\pIt suggests that you will forge a fine\npartnership with precise timing.
data/text/tv.inc.gTVNameRaterText4: A nickname fit for a unique individual\nof a POKéMON!\pIf raised properly, this POKéMON's\nuniqueness will bloom excessively!
data/text/tv.inc.gTVNameRaterText5: A nickname that will nurture the caring\nand compassionate side of POKéMON.\pIf raised properly, this POKéMON will\ncome to exhibit real warmth!
data/text/tv.inc.gTVNameRaterText6: A very fine nickname that hints at\ngreatness to come.\pI am intrigued about what the future\nholds in store for this POKéMON.
data/text/tv.inc.gTVNameRaterText7: A good nickname that should make the\nPOKéMON hale and hearty!\pThat POKéMON should remain fit and\nrobust for a long, long time.
data/text/tv.inc.gTVNameRaterText8: A good nickname that should make the\nPOKéMON very active!\pI should think that this POKéMON will be\na strong performer in battles.
data/text/tv.inc.gTVNameRaterText9: An appealing nickname that should make\nthe POKéMON very charming!\pI don't doubt that this POKéMON will be\nquite the charmer in POKéMON CONTESTS.
data/text/tv.inc.gTVNameRaterText10: The nickname {STR_VAR_1} is rooted by\nthe letter “{STR_VAR_3}.”\pThat letter is supported by the first\nletter “{STR_VAR_2},” which gives it a solid sense\lof presence as a nickname.
data/text/tv.inc.gTVNameRaterText11: The nickname {STR_VAR_1} is very\nshapely in a pleasing manner.\pThe presence of the letters “{STR_VAR_2}” and\n“{STR_VAR_3}” - now that is remarkably good!
data/text/tv.inc.gTVNameRaterText12: The nickname {STR_VAR_1} - it has a\nsublime, flowing feel to it.\pThe flow from the initial letter “{STR_VAR_2}” to\n“{STR_VAR_3}” is especially wonderful.
data/text/tv.inc.gTVNameRaterText13: Let's examine other examples of fine\nnicknames, shall we?
data/text/tv.inc.gTVNameRaterText14: Try this example. Take a part of the\nTRAINER name of {STR_VAR_1}, and end\lup with the fine nickname {STR_VAR_2}{STR_VAR_3}.
data/text/tv.inc.gTVNameRaterText15: The nickname {STR_VAR_2}{STR_VAR_3} would also work\nquite well.
data/text/tv.inc.gTVNameRaterText16: The POKéMON's species name of\n{STR_VAR_2} could be used as the basis\lfor making the nickname {STR_VAR_1}{STR_VAR_3}.
data/text/tv.inc.gTVNameRaterText18: What should always be avoided is using\nanother POKéMON species name.\pFor instance, avoid taking the name of\n{STR_VAR_2} to make the nickname {STR_VAR_1}{STR_VAR_3}.\lThat is unacceptable.
data/text/tv.inc.gTVNameRaterText19:
But I must say, {STR_VAR_2} has a most
remarkable flair for devising nicknames.\pIt is my hope that the TRAINER will
continue to treat {STR_VAR_1} with love.\pThat's it for today's show.
May we meet again.
data/text/tv.inc.gTVFishingGuruAdviceText1:
{STR_VAR_2} ANGLER\pANNOUNCER: Hello! Today, we'll get tips
on fishing for {STR_VAR_2}.\pGURU, what advice can you give for
catching {STR_VAR_2}?\pGURU: Hm? Catching {STR_VAR_2}?
Well, let me tell you, be patient and wait.\lThat's the bottom line.\pDo you see {STR_VAR_1} over there?
That TRAINER makes a good example.\pThat TRAINER's already had {STR_VAR_3}
POKéMON get away.\pBut there {STR_VAR_1} waits. No giving up.
That's the law for catching {STR_VAR_2}.\pANNOUNCER: I see...\pOh! {STR_VAR_1} has finally landed an
elusive {STR_VAR_2}!\pThe TRAINER appears close to tears
out of sheer joy!\pSeeing that elated look, I'm getting
the itch to go fishing, too!\pViewers, why not take this as a cue to
try some {STR_VAR_2} fishing?\pUntil our next broadcast, farewell and
good fishing to you all!

named {STR_VAR_1} bought {STR_VAR_3}.

important!

data/text/tv.inc.gTVSmartShopperText10:

data/text/tv.inc.gTVSmartShopperText11: CLERK: {STR_VAR_1} is a VIP customer,\nno doubt about it.
data/text/tv.inc.gTVSmartShopperText12:

data/text/tv.inc.gTVWorldOfMastersText1:
THE WORLD OF MASTERS\pHello, viewers.\pPerhaps you are aware of a TRAINER
named {STR_VAR_1}.\p{STR_VAR_1} is famous as a master at
catching POKéMON.\p{STR_VAR_1}'s quest for POKéMON depends
entirely on a careful search on foot.\pOn one memorable day, the TRAINER
walked some {STR_VAR_2} steps.\pThe total number of POKéMON caught
that day reached an impressive {STR_VAR_3}!
data/text/tv.inc.gTVWorldOfMastersText2: That remarkable feat must have been\npossible because of the trust between\lthe TRAINER and {STR_VAR_1}.
data/text/tv.inc.gTVWorldOfMastersText3:
The master caught the day's last
{STR_VAR_3} near {STR_VAR_2}.\pThat POKéMON apparently enjoys
a special status as a record holder.\pSkilled TRAINERS should be encouraged
to challenge this fine record.\pThat's all for today.
Please tune in next time.

src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_219: The key to the\nstorage inside the\nABANDONED SHIP.
src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_220: A fossil of an\nancient, seafloor-\ndwelling POKéMON.
src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_221: A fossil of an\nancient, seafloor-\ndwelling POKéMON.
src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_222: A device by DEVON\nthat signals any\nunseeable POKéMON.
src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_223: Powerful, but makes\nthe user flinch if\nhit by the foe.
src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_224: Hooks and slashes\nthe foe with long,\nsharp claws.
src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_226: Raises SP. ATK and\nSP. DEF by focusing\nthe mind.
src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_227: A savage roar that\nmakes the foe flee \nto end the battle.
src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_228: Poisons the foe\nwith a toxin that\ngradually worsens.
src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_229: Summons a hailstorm\nthat hurts all\ntypes except ICE.
src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_230: Bulks up the body\nto boost both\nATTACK & DEFENSE.
src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_231: Shoots 2 to 5 seeds\nin a row to strike\nthe foe.
src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_233: Raises the power of\nFIRE-type moves\nfor 5 turns.
src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_234: Enrages the foe so\nit can only use\nattack moves.
src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_235: Fires an icy cold\nbeam that may\nfreeze the foe.
src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_236: A vicious snow-and-\nwind attack that\nmay freeze the foe.
src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_238: Creates a wall of\nlight that lowers\nSP. ATK damage.
src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_239: Negates all damage,\nbut may fail if used\nin succession.
src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_240: Raises the power of\nWATER-type moves\nfor 5 turns.
src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_241: Recovers half the\nHP of the damage \nthis move inflicts.
src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_243: The less the user\nlikes you, the more\npowerful this move.
src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_244: Absorbs sunlight in\nthe 1st turn, then\nattacks next turn.
src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_245: Slams the foe with\na hard tail. It may\nlower DEFENSE.
src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_246: A powerful electric\nattack that may\ncause paralysis.
src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_247: Strikes the foe\nwith a thunderbolt.\nIt may paralyze.
src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_248: Causes a quake\nthat has no effect\non flying foes.
src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_249: The more the user\nlikes you, the more\npowerful this move.
src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_250: Digs underground\nthe 1st turn, then\nstrikes next turn.
src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_251: A powerful psychic\nattack that may\nlower SP. DEF.
src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_252: Hurls a dark lump\nat the foe. It may\nlower SP. DEF.
src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_253: Destroys barriers\nlike LIGHT SCREEN\nand causes damage.
src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_255: Creates a wall of\nlight that weakens\nphysical attacks.
src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_256: Zaps the foe with a\njolt of electricity\nthat never misses.
src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_257: Looses a stream of\nfire that may burn\nthe foe.
src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_258: Hurls sludge at the\nfoe. It may poison\nthe foe.
src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_259: Causes a sandstorm\nthat hits the foe\nover several turns.
src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_260: A powerful fire\nattack that may\nburn the foe.
src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_261: Stops the foe from\nmoving with rocks.\nMay lower SPEED.
src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_262: An extremely fast\nattack that can't\nbe avoided.
src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_263: Prevents the foe\nfrom using the same\nmove in a row.
src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_265: Adds an effect to\nattack depending\non the location.
src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_266: The user sleeps for\n2 turns to restore\nhealth and status.
src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_267: Makes it tough to\nattack a foe of the\nopposite gender.
src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_268: While attacking,\nit may steal the\nfoe's held item.
src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_269: Spreads hard-edged\nwings and slams\ninto the foe.
src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_270: Switches abilities\nwith the foe on the\nturn this is used.
src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_271: Steals the effects\nof the move the foe\nis trying to use.
src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_272: Enables full-power\nattack, but sharply\nlowers SP. ATK.
src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_273: Attacks the foe\nwith sharp blades\nor claws.
src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_274: Flies up on the\nfirst turn, then\nattacks next turn.
src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_275: Creates a huge\nwave, then crashes\nit down on the foe.
src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_277: Looses a powerful\nblast of light that\nreduces accuracy.
src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_278: A rock-crushingly\ntough attack that\nmay lower DEFENSE.
src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_279: Attacks the foe\nwith enough power\nto climb waterfalls.
src/data/item_descriptions_en.h.string_280: Dives underwater\nthe 1st turn, then\nattacks next turn.

src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_0: This is a newly discovered POKéMON.\nIt is currently under investigation.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1: No detailed information is available\nat this time.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_2: BULBASAUR can be seen napping in\nbright sunlight.\nThere is a seed on its back.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_3: By soaking up the sun's rays, the seed\ngrows progressively larger.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_4: There is a bud on this POKéMON's back.\nTo support its weight, IVYSAUR's legs\nand trunk grow thick and strong.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_5: If it starts spending more time lying\nin the sunlight, it's a sign that the\nbud will bloom into a large flower soon.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_6: There is a large flower on VENUSAUR's\nback. The flower is said to take on vivid\ncolors if it gets plenty of nutrition
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_7: and sunlight. The flower's aroma\nsoothes the emotions of people.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_8: The flame that burns at the tip of its\ntail is an indication of its emotions.\nThe flame wavers when CHARMANDER is
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_9: enjoying itself. If the POKéMON becomes\nenraged, the flame burns fiercely.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_10: CHARMELEON mercilessly destroys its\nfoes using its sharp claws.\nIf it encounters a strong foe, it turns
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_11: aggressive. In this excited state, the\nflame at the tip of its tail flares with a\nbluish white color.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_12: CHARIZARD flies around the sky in\nsearch of powerful opponents.\nIt breathes fire of such great heat
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_13: that it melts anything. However, it\nnever turns its fiery breath on any\nopponent weaker than itself.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_14: SQUIRTLE's shell is not merely used\nfor protection.\nThe shell's rounded shape and the
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_15: grooves on its surface help minimize\nresistance in water, enabling this\nPOKéMON to swim at high speeds.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_16: Its tail is large and covered with a rich,\nthick fur. The tail becomes increasingly\ndeeper in color as WARTORTLE ages.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_17: The scratches on its shell are evidence\nof this POKéMON's toughness as a\nbattler.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_18: BLASTOISE has water spouts that\nprotrude from its shell. The water\nspouts are very accurate.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_19: They can shoot bullets of water with\nenough accuracy to strike empty cans\nfrom a distance of over 160 feet.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_20: CATERPIE has a voracious appetite.\nIt can devour leaves bigger than its\nbody right before your eyes.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_21: From its antenna, this POKéMON releases\na terrifically strong odor.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_22: The shell covering this POKéMON's body\nis as hard as an iron slab.\nMETAPOD does not move very much.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_23: It stays still because it is preparing\nits soft innards for evolution inside\nthe hard shell.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_24: BUTTERFREE has a superior ability to\nsearch for delicious honey from\nflowers.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_25: It can even search out, extract, and\ncarry honey from flowers that are\nblooming over six miles from its nest.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_26: WEEDLE has an extremely acute sense\nof smell.\nIt is capable of distinguishing its
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_27: favorite kinds of leaves from those it\ndislikes just by sniffing with its big\nred proboscis (nose).
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_28: KAKUNA remains virtually immobile as it\nclings to a tree. However, on the\ninside, it is extremely busy as it
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_29: prepares for its coming evolution.\nThis is evident from how hot the shell\nbecomes to the touch.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_30: BEEDRILL is extremely territorial.\nNo one should ever approach its nest -\nthis is for their own safety.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_31: If angered, they will attack in a furious\nswarm.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_32: PIDGEY has an extremely sharp sense\nof direction.\nIt is capable of unerringly returning
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_33: home to its nest, however far it may be\nremoved from its familiar surroundings.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_34: PIDGEOTTO claims a large area as its\nown territory. This POKéMON flies\naround, patrolling its living space.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_35: If its territory is violated, it shows\nno mercy in thoroughly punishing the\nfoe with its sharp claws.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_36: This POKéMON has a dazzling plumage of\nbeautifully glossy feathers.\nMany TRAINERS are captivated by the
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_37: striking beauty of the feathers on its\nhead, compelling them to choose PIDGEOT\nas their POKéMON.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_38: RATTATA is cautious in the extreme.\nEven while it is asleep, it constantly\nlistens by moving its ears around.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_39: It is not picky about where it lives -\nit will make its nest anywhere.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_40: RATICATE's sturdy fangs grow steadily.\nTo keep them ground down, it gnaws\non rocks and logs.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_41: It may even chew on the walls of\nhouses.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_42: SPEAROW has a very loud cry that can\nbe heard over half a mile away.\nIf its high, keening cry is heard
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_43: echoing all around, it is a sign that\nthey are warning each other of danger.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_44: FEAROW is recognized by its long neck\nand elongated beak.\nThey are conveniently shaped for
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_45: catching prey in soil or water.\nIt deftly moves its long and skinny\nbeak to pluck prey.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_46: EKANS curls itself up in a spiral while\nit rests.\nAssuming this position allows it to
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_47: quickly respond to a threat from any\ndirection with a glare from its upraised\nhead.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_48: This POKéMON is terrifically strong in\norder to constrict things with its body.\nIt can even flatten steel oil drums.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_49: Once ARBOK wraps its body around its\nfoe, escaping its crunching embrace is\nimpossible.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_50: This POKéMON has electricity-storing\npouches on its cheeks. These appear to\nbecome electrically charged during the
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_51: night while PIKACHU sleeps.\nIt occasionally discharges electricity\nwhen it is dozy after waking up.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_52: Whenever PIKACHU comes across\nsomething new, it blasts it with a jolt\nof electricity.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_53: If you come across a blackened berry,\nit's evidence that this POKéMON\nmistook the intensity of its charge.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_54: This POKéMON exudes a weak electrical\ncharge from all over its body that makes\nit take on a slight glow in darkness.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_55: RAICHU searches for electricity by\nplanting its tail in the ground.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_56: If the electrical sacks become\nexcessively charged, RAICHU plants its\ntail in the ground and discharges.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_57: Scorched patches of ground will be\nfound near this POKéMON's nest.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_58: SANDSHREW has a very dry hide that is\nextremely tough. The POKéMON can roll\ninto a ball that repels any attack.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_59: At night, it burrows into the desert\nsand to sleep.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_60: SANDSHREW's body is configured to\nabsorb water without waste, enabling it\nto survive in an arid desert.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_61: This POKéMON curls up to protect itself\nfrom its enemies.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_62: SANDSLASH can roll up its body as if it\nwere a ball covered with large spikes.\nIn battle, this POKéMON will try to make
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_63: the foe flinch by jabbing it with its\nspines. It then leaps at the stunned\nfoe to tear wildly with its sharp claws.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_64: SANDSLASH's body is covered by tough\nspikes, which are hardened sections of\nits hide. Once a year, the old spikes fall
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_65: out, to be replaced with new spikes that\ngrow out from beneath the old ones.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_66: NIDORAN has barbs that secrete a\npowerful poison. They are thought to\nhave developed as protection for this
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_67: small-bodied POKéMON.\nWhen enraged, it releases a horrible\ntoxin from its horn.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_68: When NIDORINA are with their friends or\nfamily, they keep their barbs tucked\naway to prevent hurting each other.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_69: This POKéMON appears to become\nnervous if separated from the others.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_70: NIDOQUEEN's body is encased in\nextremely hard scales. It is adept at\nsending foes flying with harsh tackles.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_71: This POKéMON is at its strongest when\nit is defending its young.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_72: The male NIDORAN has developed\nmuscles for moving its ears. Thanks to\nthem, the ears can be freely moved in
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_73: any direction. Even the slightest sound\ndoes not escape this POKéMON's notice.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_74: NIDORINO has a horn that is harder than\na diamond. If it senses a hostile\npresence, all the barbs on its back
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_75: bristle up at once, and it challenges\nthe foe with all its might.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_76: NIDOKING's thick tail packs enormously\ndestructive power. With one swing, it\ncan topple a metal transmission tower.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_77: Once this POKéMON goes on a rampage,\nthere is no stopping it.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_78: On every night of a full moon, groups of\nthis POKéMON come out to play.\nWhen dawn arrives, the tired CLEFAIRY
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_79: return to their quiet mountain retreats\nand go to sleep nestled up against each\nother.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_80: CLEFABLE moves by skipping lightly as if\nit were flying using its wings. Its\nbouncy step lets it even walk on water.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_81: It is known to take strolls on lakes on\nquiet, moonlit nights.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_82: Inside VULPIX's body burns a flame that\nnever goes out. During the daytime,\nwhen the temperatures rise, this
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_83: POKéMON releases flames from its mouth\nto prevent its body from growing too\nhot.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_84: At the time of its birth, VULPIX has one\nwhite tail. The tail separates into six\nif this POKéMON receives plenty of love
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_85: from its TRAINER.\nThe six tails become magnificently\ncurled.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_86: Legend has it that NINETALES came into\nbeing when nine wizards possessing\nsacred powers merged into one.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_87: This POKéMON is highly intelligent - it\ncan understand human speech.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_88: NINETALES casts a sinister light from\nits bright red eyes to gain total\ncontrol over its foe's mind.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_89: This POKéMON is said to live for a\nthousand years.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_90: When this POKéMON sings, it never\npauses to breathe. If it is in a battle\nagainst an opponent that does not
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_91: easily fall asleep, JIGGLYPUFF cannot\nbreathe, endangering its life.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_92: JIGGLYPUFF's vocal chords can freely\nadjust the wavelength of its voice.\nThis POKéMON uses this ability to sing
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_93: at precisely the right wavelength to\nmake its foes most drowsy.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_94: WIGGLYTUFF's body is very flexible.\nBy inhaling deeply, this POKéMON can\ninflate itself seemingly without end.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_95: Once inflated, WIGGLYTUFF bounces\nalong lightly like a balloon.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_96: WIGGLYTUFF has large, saucerlike eyes.\nThe surfaces of its eyes are always\ncovered with a thin layer of tears.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_97: If any dust gets in this POKéMON's\neyes, it is quickly washed away.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_98: ZUBAT avoids sunlight because exposure\ncauses it to become unhealthy.\nDuring the daytime, it stays in caves or
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_99: under the eaves of old houses, sleeping\nwhile hanging upside down.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_100: ZUBAT remains quietly unmoving in a\ndark spot during the bright daylight\nhours. It does so because prolonged
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_101: exposure to the sun causes its body to\nbecome slightly burned.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_102: GOLBAT bites down on prey with its four\nfangs and drinks the victim's blood.\nIt becomes active on inky dark
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_103: moonless nights, flying around to\nattack people and POKéMON.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_104: GOLBAT loves to drink the blood of\nliving things. It is particularly active\nin the pitch black of night.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_105: This POKéMON flits around in the night\nskies, seeking fresh blood.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_106: ODDISH searches for fertile, nutrient-\nrich soil, then plants itself.\nDuring the daytime, while it is planted,
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_107: this POKéMON's feet are thought to\nchange shape and become similar to\nthe roots of trees.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_108: During the daytime, ODDISH buries\nitself in soil to absorb nutrients from \nthe ground using its entire body.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_109: The more fertile the soil, the glossier\nits leaves become.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_110: From its mouth GLOOM drips honey that\nsmells absolutely horrible.\nApparently, it loves the horrid stench.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_111: It sniffs the noxious fumes and then \ndrools even more of its honey.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_112: GLOOM releases a foul fragrance from\nthe pistil of its flower. When faced\nwith danger, the stench worsens.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_113: If this POKéMON is feeling calm and\nsecure, it does not release its usual\nstinky aroma.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_114: VILEPLUME has the world's largest\npetals. They are used to attract prey\nthat are then doused with toxic spores.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_115: Once the prey are immobilized, this\nPOKéMON catches and devours them.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_116: VILEPLUME's toxic pollen triggers\natrocious allergy attacks. That's why\nit is advisable never to approach any
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_117: attractive flowers in a jungle, however\npretty they may be.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_118: PARAS has parasitic mushrooms growing\non its back called tochukaso. They grow\nlarge by drawing nutrients from this
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_120: highly valued as a medicine for\nextending life.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_121: PARAS has parasitic mushrooms growing\non its back called tochukaso. They grow\nlarge by drawing nutrients from this
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_122: BUG POKéMON host. They are highly\nvalued as a medicine for extending life.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_123: PARAS has parasitic mushrooms growing\non its back called tochukaso. They grow\nlarge by drawing nutrients from this
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_124: BUG/GRASS POKéMON host. They are\nhighly valued as a medicine for\nextending life.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_125: PARAS has parasitic mushrooms growing\non its back called tochukaso. They grow\nlarge by drawing nutrients from the BUG
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_126: POKéMON host. They are highly valued as\na medicine for extending life.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_127: PARASECT is known to infest large trees\nen masse and drain nutrients from the\nlower trunk and roots.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_128: When an infested tree dies, they move\nonto another tree all at once.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_129: VENONAT is said to have evolved with\na coat of thin, stiff hair that covers\nits entire body for protection.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_130: It possesses large eyes that never fail\nto spot even miniscule prey.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_131: VENOMOTH is nocturnal - it is a POKéMON\nthat only becomes active at night.\nIts favorite prey are small insects
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_132: that gather around streetlights,\nattracted by the light in the darkness.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_133: DIGLETT are raised in most farms.\nThe reason is simple - wherever this\nPOKéMON burrows, the soil is left
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_134: perfectly tilled for planting crops.\nThis soil is made ideal for growing\ndelicious vegetables.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_135: DUGTRIO are actually triplets that\nemerged from one body. As a result,\neach triplet thinks exactly like the
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_136: other two triplets.\nThey work cooperatively to burrow\nendlessly.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_137: MEOWTH withdraws its sharp claws into\nits paws to slinkily sneak about without\nmaking any incriminating footsteps.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_138: For some reason, this POKéMON loves\nshiny coins that glitter with light.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_139: PERSIAN has six bold whiskers that give\nit a look of toughness. The whiskers  \nsense air movements to determine what
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_140: is in the POKéMON's surrounding\nvicinity. It becomes docile if grabbed\nby the whiskers.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_141: If it uses its mysterious power,\nPSYDUCK can't remember having done so.\nIt apparently can't form a memory of
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_142: such an event because it goes into\nan altered state that is much like\ndeep sleep.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_143: PSYDUCK uses a mysterious power.\nWhen it does so, this POKéMON \ngenerates brain waves that are
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_144: supposedly only seen in sleepers.\nThis discovery spurred controversy\namong scholars.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_145: GOLDUCK is the fastest swimmer among\nall POKéMON. It swims effortlessly, even\nin a rough, stormy sea.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_146: It sometimes rescues people from\nwrecked ships floundering in high seas.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_147: The webbed flippers on its forelegs and\nhind legs and the streamlined body of\nGOLDUCK give it frightening speed.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_148: This POKéMON is definitely much faster\nthan even the most athletic swimmer.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_149: When MANKEY starts shaking and its\nnasal breathing turns rough, it's a sure\nsign that it is becoming angry.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_150: However, because it goes into a\ntowering rage almost instantly, it is\nimpossible for anyone to flee its wrath.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_151: When PRIMEAPE becomes furious, its\nblood circulation is boosted. In turn,\nits muscles are made even stronger.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_152: However, it also becomes much less\nintelligent at the same time.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_153: GROWLITHE has a superb sense of smell.\nOnce it smells anything, this POKéMON\nwon't forget the scent, no matter what.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_154: It uses its advanced olfactory sense\nto determine the emotions of other\nliving things.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_155: ARCANINE is known for its high speed.\nIt is said to be capable of running over\n6,200 miles in a single day and night.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_156: The fire that blazes wildly within this\nPOKéMON's body is its source of power.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_157: POLIWAG has a very thin skin. It is\npossible to see the POKéMON's spiral\ninnards right through the skin.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_158: Despite its thinness, however, the skin\nis also very flexible. Even sharp fangs\nbounce right off it.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_159: The surface of POLIWHIRL's body is\nalways wet and slick with an oily fluid.\nBecause of this greasy covering, it can
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_160: easily slip and slide out of the clutches\nof any enemy in battle.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_161: POLIWRATH's highly developed, brawny\nmuscles never grow fatigued, however\nmuch it exercises.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_162: It is so tirelessly strong, this POKéMON\ncan swim back and forth across the\nPacific Ocean without effort.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_163: ABRA needs to sleep for eighteen hours\na day. If it doesn't, this POKéMON loses\nits ability to use telekinetic powers.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_164: If it is attacked, ABRA escapes using\nTELEPORT while it is still sleeping.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_165: ABRA sleeps for eighteen hours a day.\nHowever, it can sense the presence of\nfoes even while it is sleeping.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_166: In such a situation, this POKéMON\nimmediately teleports to safety.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_167: KADABRA holds a silver spoon in its\nhand. The spoon is used to amplify the\nalpha waves in its brain.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_168: Without the spoon, the POKéMON is said\nto be limited to half the usual amount\nof its telekinetic powers.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_169: KADABRA emits a peculiar alpha wave\nif it develops a headache. Only those\npeople with a particularly strong
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_170: psyche can hope to become a TRAINER\nof this POKéMON.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_171: ALAKAZAM's brain continually grows,\ninfinitely multiplying brain cells.\nThis amazing brain gives this POKéMON
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_172: an astoundingly high IQ of 5,000.\nIt has a thorough memory of everything\nthat has occurred in the world.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_173: ALAKAZAM's brain continually grows,\nmaking its head far too heavy to\nsupport with its neck.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_174: This POKéMON holds its head up using\nits psychokinetic power instead.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_175: MACHOP exercises by hefting around\na GRAVELER as if it were a barbell.\nThere are some MACHOP that travel
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_176: the world in a quest to master all\nkinds of martial arts.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_177: MACHOP's muscles are special - they\nnever get sore no matter how much they\nare used in exercise.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_178: This POKéMON has sufficient power to\nhurl a hundred adult humans.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_179: MACHOKE undertakes bodybuilding every\nday even as it helps people with tough,\nphysically demanding labor.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_180: On its days off, this POKéMON heads to\nthe fields and mountains to exercise\nand train.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_181: MACHOKE's thoroughly toned muscles\npossess the hardness of steel.\nThis POKéMON has so much strength,
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_182: it can easily hold aloft a sumo wrestler\non just one finger.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_183: MACHAMP is known as the POKéMON that\nhas mastered every kind of martial arts.\nIf it grabs hold of the foe with its four
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_184: arms, the battle is all but over.\nThe hapless foe is thrown far over the\nhorizon.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_185: MACHAMP has the power to hurl anything\naside. However, trying to do any work\nrequiring care and dexterity causes
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_186: its arms to get tangled.\nThis POKéMON tends to leap into action\nbefore it thinks.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_187: BELLSPROUT's thin and flexible body\nlets it bend and sway to avoid any\nattack, however strong it may be.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_188: From its mouth, this POKéMON spits a\ncorrosive fluid that melts even iron.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_189: WEEPINBELL has a large hook on its rear\nend. At night, the POKéMON hooks on to\na tree branch and goes to sleep.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_190: If it moves around in its sleep, it may\nwake up to find itself on the ground.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_191: VICTREEBEL has a long vine that\nextends from its head. This vine is\nwaved and flicked about as if it were
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_192: an animal to attract prey. When an\nunsuspecting prey draws near, this\nPOKéMON swallows it whole.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_193: TENTACOOL absorbs sunlight and\nrefracts it using water inside its body\nto convert it into beam energy.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_194: This POKéMON shoots beams from its\ncrystal-like eyes.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_195: TENTACOOL's body is largely composed\nof water. If it is removed from the\nsea, it dries up like parchment.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_196: If this POKéMON happens to become\ndehydrated, put it back into the sea.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_197: TENTACRUEL has tentacles that can be\nfreely elongated and shortened at will.\nIt ensnares prey with its tentacles and
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_198: weakens the prey by dosing it with a\nharsh toxin. It can catch up to 80\nprey at the same time.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_199: TENTACRUEL has large red orbs on its\nhead. The orbs glow before lashing the\nvicinity with a harsh ultrasonic blast.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_200: This POKéMON's outburst creates rough\nwaves around it.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_201: When GEODUDE sleeps deeply, it buries\nitself halfway into the ground.\nIt will not awaken even if hikers step
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_202: on it unwittingly.\nIn the morning, this POKéMON rolls\ndownhill in search of food.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_203: The longer a GEODUDE lives, the more\nits edges are chipped and worn away,\nmaking it more rounded in appearance.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_204: However, this POKéMON's heart will\nremain hard, craggy, and rough always.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_205: Rocks are GRAVELER's favorite food.\nThis POKéMON will climb a mountain from\nthe base to the summit, crunchingly
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_206: feasting on rocks all the while.\nUpon reaching the peak, it rolls back\ndown to the bottom.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_207: GRAVELER grows by feeding on rocks.\nApparently, it prefers to eat rocks\nthat are covered in moss.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_208: This POKéMON eats its way through\na ton of rocks on a daily basis.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_209: GOLEM is known for rolling down from\nmountains. To prevent them from rolling\ninto the homes of people downhill,
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_210: grooves have been dug into the sides of\nmountains to serve as guideways for\ndiverting this POKéMON's course.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_211: GOLEM live up on mountains.\nIf there is a large earthquake, these\nPOKéMON will come rolling down off
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_212: the mountains en masse to the\nfoothills below.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_213: PONYTA is very weak at birth.\nIt can barely stand up.\nThis POKéMON becomes stronger by
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_214: stumbling and falling to keep up with\nits parent.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_215: RAPIDASH usually can be seen casually\ncantering in the fields and plains.\nHowever, when this POKéMON turns
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_216: serious, its fiery manes flare and blaze\nas it gallops its way up to 150 mph.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_217: SLOWPOKE uses its tail to catch prey by\ndipping it in water at the side of a\nriver.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_218: However, this POKéMON often forgets\nwhat it's doing and often spends entire\ndays just loafing at water's edge.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_219: SLOWBRO's tail has a SHELLDER firmly\nattached with a bite. As a result, the\ntail can't be used for fishing anymore.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_220: This causes SLOWBRO to grudgingly swim\nand catch prey instead.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_221: MAGNEMITE floats in the air by emitting\nelectromagnetic waves from the units\nat its sides. These waves block gravity.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_222: This POKéMON becomes incapable of\nflight if its internal electrical supply\nis depleted.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_223: MAGNEMITE attaches itself to power\nlines to feed on electricity.\nIf your house has a power outage,
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_224: check your circuit breakers. You may\nfind a large number of this POKéMON\nclinging to the breaker box.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_225: MAGNETON emits a powerful magnetic\nforce that is fatal to electronics and\nprecision instruments. Because of
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_226: this, it is said that some towns warn\npeople to keep this POKéMON inside\na POKé BALL.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_227: MAGNETON emits a powerful magnetic\nforce that is fatal to mechanical\ndevices. As a result, large cities sound
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_228: sirens to warn citizens of large-scale\noutbreaks of this POKéMON.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_229: FARFETCH'D is always seen with a stick\nfrom a plant of some sort. Apparently,\nthere are good sticks and bad sticks.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_230: This POKéMON has been known to fight\nwith others over sticks.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_231: DODUO's two heads contain completely\nidentical brains.\nA scientific study reported that on
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_232: rare occasions, there will be examples\nof this POKéMON possessing different\nsets of brains.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_233: DODUO's two heads never sleep at the\nsame time.\nIts two heads take turns sleeping,
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_234: so one head can always keep watch for\nenemies while the other one sleeps.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_235: Apparently, the heads aren't the only\nparts of the body that DODRIO has\nthree of.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_236: It has three sets of hearts and lungs\nas well, so it is capable of running long\ndistances without rest.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_237: Watch out if DODRIO's three heads are\nlooking in three separate directions.\nIt's a sure sign that it is on its guard.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_238: Don't go near this POKéMON if it's being\nwary - it may decide to peck you.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_239: SEEL hunts for prey in the frigid sea\nunderneath sheets of ice.\nWhen it needs to breathe, it punches
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_240: a hole through the ice with the\nsharply protruding section of its head.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_241: DEWGONG loves to snooze on bitterly\ncold ice.\nThe sight of this POKéMON sleeping on
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_242: a glacier was mistakenly thought to be\na mermaid by a mariner long ago.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_243: GRIMER emerged from the sludge that\nsettled on a polluted seabed.\nThis POKéMON loves anything filthy.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_244: It constantly leaks a horribly germ-\ninfested fluid from all over its body.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_245: GRIMER's sludgy and rubbery body can\nbe forced through any opening, however\nsmall it may be.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_246: This POKéMON enters sewer pipes to\ndrink filthy wastewater.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_247: This POKéMON's favorite food is\nanything that is repugnantly filthy.\nIn dirty towns where people think
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_248: nothing of throwing away litter on the\nstreets, MUK are certain to gather.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_249: From MUK's body seeps a foul fluid that\ngives off a nose-bendingly horrible\nstench.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_250: Just one drop of this POKéMON's body\nfluid can turn a pool stagnant and\nrancid.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_251: At night, this POKéMON uses its broad\ntongue to burrow a hole in the seafloor\nsand and then sleep in it.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_252: While it is sleeping, SHELLDER closes its\nshell, but leaves its tongue hanging\nout.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_253: CLOYSTER is capable of swimming in the\nsea. It does so by swallowing water,\nthen jetting it out toward the rear.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_254: This POKéMON shoots spikes from its\nshell using the same system.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_255: GASTLY is largely composed of gaseous\nmatter. When exposed to a strong wind,\nthe gaseous body quickly dwindles away.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_256: Groups of this POKéMON cluster under\nthe eaves of houses to escape the\nravages of wind.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_257: HAUNTER is a dangerous POKéMON.\nIf one beckons you while floating in\ndarkness, you must never approach it.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_258: This POKéMON will try to lick you with its\ntongue and steal your life away.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_259: Sometimes, on a dark night, your shadow\nthrown by a streetlight will suddenly\nand startlingly overtake you.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_260: It is actually a GENGAR running past\nyou, pretending to be your shadow.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_261: ONIX has a magnet in its brain. It acts\nas a compass so that this POKéMON does\nnot lose direction while it is tunneling.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_262: As it grows older, its body becomes\nincreasingly rounder and smoother.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_263: If your nose becomes itchy while you\nare sleeping, it's a sure sign that one\nof these POKéMON is standing above
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_264: your pillow and trying to eat your dream\nthrough your nostrils.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_265: HYPNO holds a pendulum in its hand.\nThe arcing movement and glitter of the\npendulum lull the foe into a deep state
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_266: of hypnosis.\nWhile this POKéMON searches for prey,\nit polishes the pendulum.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_267: KRABBY live on beaches, burrowed inside\nholes dug into the sand.\nOn sandy beaches with little in the way
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_268: of food, these POKéMON can be seen\nsquabbling with each other over\nterritory.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_269: KINGLER has an enormous, oversized\nclaw. It waves this huge claw in the\nair to communicate with others.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_270: However, because the claw is so heavy,\nthe POKéMON quickly tires.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_271: VOLTORB is extremely sensitive - it\nexplodes at the slightest of shocks.\nIt is rumored that it was first created
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_272: when a POKé BALL was exposed to a\npowerful pulse of energy.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_273: VOLTORB was first sighted at a company\nthat manufactures POKé BALLS.\nThe link between that sighting and
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_274: the fact that this POKéMON looks very\nsimilar to a POKé BALL remains a\nmystery.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_275: One of ELECTRODE's characteristics is\nits attraction to electricity.\nIt is a problematical POKéMON that
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_276: congregates mostly at electrical\npower plants to feed on electricity\nthat has just been generated.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_277: ELECTRODE eats electricity in the\natmosphere. On days when lightning\nstrikes, you can see this POKéMON
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_278: exploding all over the place from\neating too much electricity.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_279: This POKéMON consists of six eggs that\nform a closely knit cluster. The six eggs\nattract each other and spin around.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_280: When cracks increasingly appear on the\neggs, EXEGGCUTE is close to evolution.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_281: EXEGGUTOR originally came from the\ntropics. Its heads steadily grow larger\nfrom exposure to strong sunlight.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_282: It is said that when the heads fall off,\nthey group together to form EXEGGCUTE.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_283: CUBONE pines for the mother it will\nnever see again. Seeing a likeness of\nits mother in the full moon, it cries.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_284: The stains on the skull the POKéMON\nwears are made by the tears it sheds.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_285: MAROWAK is the evolved form of a CUBONE\nthat has overcome its sadness at the\nloss of its mother and grown tough.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_286: This POKéMON's tempered and hardened\nspirit is not easily broken.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_287: HITMONLEE's legs freely contract and\nstretch. Using these springlike legs, it\nbowls over foes with devastating kicks.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_288: After battle, it rubs down its legs and\nloosens the muscles to overcome\nfatigue.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_289: HITMONCHAN is said to possess the\nspirit of a boxer who had been working\ntowards a world championship.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_290: This POKéMON has an indomitable spirit\nand will never give up in the face of\nadversity.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_291: Whenever LICKITUNG comes across\nsomething new, it will unfailingly give it\na lick. It does so because it memorizes
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_292: things by texture and by taste.\nIt is somewhat put off by sour things.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_293: KOFFING embodies toxic substances.\nIt mixes the toxins with raw garbage to\nset off a chemical reaction that
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_294: results in a terribly powerful poison\ngas. The higher the temperature, the\nmore gas is concocted by this POKéMON.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_295: If KOFFING becomes agitated, it raises\nthe toxicity of its internal gases and\njets them out from all over its body.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_296: This POKéMON may also overinflate its\nround body, then explode.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_297: WEEZING alternately shrinks and\ninflates its twin bodies to mix together\ntoxic gases inside.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_298: The more the gases are mixed, the more\npowerful the toxins become. The\nPOKéMON also becomes more putrid.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_299: WEEZING loves the gases given off by\nrotted kitchen garbage. This POKéMON\nwill find a dirty, unkempt house and
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_300: make it its home. At night, when the\npeople in the house are asleep, it will\ngo through the trash.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_301: RHYHORN's brain is very small. It is so\ndense, while on a run it forgets why it\nstarted running in the first place.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_302: It apparently remembers sometimes if it\ndemolishes something.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_303: RHYHORN runs in a straight line,\nsmashing everything in its path.\nIt is not bothered even if it rushes
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_304: headlong into a block of steel.\nThis POKéMON may feel some pain from\nthe collision the next day, however.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_305: RHYDON has a horn that serves as a\ndrill. It is used for destroying rocks\nand boulders.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_306: This POKéMON occasionally rams into\nstreams of magma, but the armor-like\nhide prevents it from feeling the heat.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_307: RHYDON's horn can crush even uncut\ndiamonds. One sweeping blow of its tail\ncan topple a building.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_308: This POKéMON's hide is extremely tough.\nEven direct cannon hits don't leave\na scratch.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_309: CHANSEY lays nutritionally excellent\neggs on an everyday basis.\nThe eggs are so delicious, they are
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_310: easily and eagerly devoured by even\nthose people who have lost their\nappetite.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_311: TANGELA's vines snap off easily if they\nare grabbed. This happens without pain,\nallowing it to make a quick getaway.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_312: The lost vines are replaced by newly\ngrown vines the very next day.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_313: If you come across a young KANGASKHAN\nplaying by itself, you must never\ndisturb it or attempt to catch it.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_314: The baby POKéMON's parent is sure to\nbe in the area, and it will become\nviolently enraged at you.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_315: If HORSEA senses danger, it will\nreflexively spray a dense black ink\nfrom its mouth and try to escape.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_316: This POKéMON swims by cleverly flapping\nthe fins on its back.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_317: HORSEA eats small insects and moss off\nof rocks. If the ocean current turns\nfast, this POKéMON anchors itself by
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_318: wrapping its tail around rocks or coral\nto prevent being washed away.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_319: SEADRA generates whirlpools by spinning\nits body. The whirlpools are strong\nenough to swallow even fishing boats.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_320: This POKéMON weakens prey with these\ncurrents, then swallows it whole.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_321: SEADRA sleeps after wriggling itself\nbetween the branches of coral.\nThose trying to harvest coral are
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_322: occasionally stung by this POKéMON's\npoison barbs if they fail to notice it.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_323: GOLDEEN loves swimming wild and free\nin rivers and ponds.\nIf one of these POKéMON is placed in an
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_324: aquarium, it will shatter even the\nthickest glass with one ram of its horn\nand make its escape.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_325: GOLDEEN is a very beautiful POKéMON\nwith fins that billow elegantly in water.\nHowever, don't let your guard down
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_326: around this POKéMON - it could ram you\npowerfully with its horn.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_327: SEAKING is very protective of its eggs.\nThe male and female will take turns\npatrolling around their nest and eggs.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_328: The guarding of eggs by these POKéMON\ngoes on for over a month.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_329: In the autumn, SEAKING males can be\nseen performing courtship dances in\nriverbeds to woo females.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_330: During this season, this POKéMON's body\ncoloration is at its most beautiful.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_331: STARYU apparently communicates with\nthe stars in the night sky by flashing\nthe red core at the center of its body.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_332: If parts of its body are torn, this\nPOKéMON simply regenerates the\nmissing pieces and limbs.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_333: STARYU's center section has an organ\ncalled the core that shines bright red.\nIf you go to a beach toward the
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_334: end of summer, the glowing cores of\nthese POKéMON look like the stars\nin the sky.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_335: STARMIE swims through water by\nspinning its star-shaped body as if it\nwere a propeller on a ship.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_336: The core at the center of this\nPOKéMON's body glows in seven colors.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_337: STARMIE's center section - the core -\nglows brightly in seven colors.\nBecause of its luminous nature, this
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_338: POKéMON has been given the nickname\n“the gem of the sea.”
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_339: MR. MIME is a master of pantomime.\nIts gestures and motions convince\nwatchers that something unseeable
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_340: actually exists. Once it is believed,\nit will exist as if it were a real thing.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_341: SCYTHER is blindingly fast. Its blazing\nspeed enhances the effectiveness of\nthe twin scythes on its forearms.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_342: This POKéMON's scythes are so\neffective, they can slice through thick\nlogs in one wicked stroke.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_343: JYNX walks rhythmically, swaying and\nshaking its hips as if it were dancing.\nIts motions are so bouncingly alluring,
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_344: people seeing it are compelled to shake\ntheir hips without giving any thought\nto what they are doing.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_345: When a storm arrives, gangs of this\nPOKéMON compete with each other to\nscale heights that are likely to be
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_346: stricken by lightning bolts.\nSome towns use ELECTABUZZ in place of\nlightning rods.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_347: In battle, MAGMAR blows out intensely\nhot flames from all over its body to\nintimidate its opponent.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_348: This POKéMON's fiery bursts create\nheat waves that ignite grass and trees\nin its surroundings.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_349: PINSIR has a pair of massive horns \nProtruding from the surface of these\nhorns are thorns.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_350: These thorns are driven deeply into the\nfoe's body when the pincer closes,\nmaking it tough for the foe to escape.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_351: PINSIR is astoundingly strong. It can\ngrip a foe weighing twice its weight\nin its horns and easily lift it.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_352: This POKéMON's movements turn sluggish\nin cold places.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_353: This POKéMON is not satisfied unless\nit is rampaging at all times.\nIf there is no opponent for TAUROS to
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_354: battle, it will charge at thick trees and\nknock them down to calm itself.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_355: MAGIKARP is virtually useless in battle\nas it can only splash around.\nAs a result, it is considered to be weak.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_356: However, it is actually a very hardy\nPOKéMON that can survive in any body of\nwater no matter how polluted it is.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_357: MAGIKARP is a pathetic excuse for a\nPOKéMON that is only capable of\nflopping and splashing.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_358: This behavior prompted scientists to\nundertake research into it.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_359: Once GYARADOS goes on a rampage, its\nferociously violent blood doesn't calm\nuntil it has burned everything down.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_360: There are records of this POKéMON's\nrampages lasting a whole month.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_361: When MAGIKARP evolves into GYARADOS,\nits brain cells undergo a structural\ntransformation. It is said that this
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_362: transformation is to blame for this\nPOKéMON's wildly violent nature.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_363: People have driven LAPRAS almost to the\npoint of extinction. In the evenings,\nthis POKéMON is said to sing plaintively
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_364: as it seeks what few others of its kind\nstill remain.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_365: DITTO rearranges its cell structure to\ntransform itself into other shapes.\nHowever, if it tries to transform itself
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_366: into something by relying on its memory,\nthis POKéMON manages to get details\nwrong.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_367: EEVEE has an unstable genetic makeup\nthat suddenly mutates due to the\nenvironment in which it lives.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_368: Radiation from various STONES causes\nthis POKéMON to evolve.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_369: VAPOREON underwent a spontaneous\nmutation and grew fins and gills that\nallow it to live underwater.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_370: This POKéMON has the ability to freely\ncontrol water.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_371: JOLTEON's cells generate a low level of\nelectricity. This power is amplified by\nthe static electricity of its fur,
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_372: enabling the POKéMON to drop\nthunderbolts. The bristling fur is made\nof electrically charged needles.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_373: FLAREON's fluffy fur has a functional\npurpose - it releases heat into the air\nso that its body does not get
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_374: excessively hot.\nThis POKéMON's body temperature can\nrise to a maximum of 1,650 degrees F.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_375: PORYGON is capable of reverting itself\nentirely back to program data and\nentering cyberspace.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_376: This POKéMON is copy-protected so it\ncannot be duplicated by copying.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_377: OMANYTE is one of the ancient and long-\nsince-extinct POKéMON that have been\nregenerated from fossils by people.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_378: If attacked by an enemy, it withdraws\nitself inside its hard shell.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_379: OMASTAR uses its tentacles to capture\nits prey. It is believed to have become\nextinct because its shell grew too large
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_380: and heavy, causing its movements to\nbecome too slow and ponderous.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_381: KABUTO is a POKéMON that has been\nregenerated from a fossil. However, in\nextremely rare cases, living examples
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_382: have been discovered.\nThe POKéMON has not changed at all for\n300 million years.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_383: KABUTOPS swam underwater to hunt for\nits prey in ancient times.\nThe POKéMON was apparently evolving
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_384: from being a water-dweller to living on\nland as evident from the beginnings of\nchange in its gills and legs.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_385: AERODACTYL is a POKéMON from the age\nof dinosaurs. It was regenerated from\ngenetic material extracted from amber.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_386: It is imagined to have been the king of\nthe skies in ancient times.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_387: SNORLAX's typical day consists of\nnothing more than eating and sleeping.\nIt is such a docile POKéMON that there
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_388: are children who use its expansive belly\nas a place to play.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_389: ARTICUNO is a legendary bird POKéMON\nthat can control ice.\nThe flapping of its wings chills the air.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_390: As a result, it is said that when this\nPOKéMON flies, snow will fall.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_391: ZAPDOS is a legendary bird POKéMON that\nhas the ability to control electricity.\nIt usually lives in thunderclouds.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_392: The POKéMON gains power if it is\nstricken by lightning bolts.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_393: MOLTRES is a legendary bird POKéMON\nthat has the ability to control fire.\nIf this POKéMON is injured, it is said to
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_394: dip its body in the molten magma of a\nvolcano to burn and heal itself.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_395: DRATINI continually molts and sloughs\noff its old skin.\nIt does so because the life energy
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_396: within its body steadily builds to reach\nuncontrollable levels.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_397: DRAGONAIR stores an enormous amount\nof energy inside its body.\nIt is said to alter weather conditions
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_398: in its vicinity by discharging energy\nfrom the crystals on its neck and tail.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_399: DRAGONITE is capable of circling the\nglobe in just sixteen hours.\nIt is a kindhearted POKéMON that leads
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_400: lost and foundering ships in a storm to\nthe safety of land.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_401: MEWTWO is a POKéMON that was created\nby genetic manipulation.\nHowever, even though the scientific
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_402: power of humans created this POKéMON's\nbody, they failed to endow MEWTWO with\na compassionate heart.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_403: MEW is said to possess the genetic\ncomposition of all POKéMON.\nIt is capable of making itself invisible
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_404: at will, so it entirely avoids notice even\nif it approaches people.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_405: In battle, CHIKORITA waves its leaf\naround to keep the foe at bay.\nHowever, a sweet fragrance also wafts
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_406: from the leaf, becalming the battling\nPOKéMON and creating a cozy, friendly\natmosphere all around.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_407: BAYLEEF's neck is ringed by curled-up\nleaves. Inside each tubular leaf is a\nsmall shoot of a tree.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_408: The fragrance of this shoot makes\npeople peppy.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_409: The fragrance of MEGANIUM's flower\nsoothes and calms emotions.\nIn battle, this POKéMON gives off more
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_410: of its becalming scent to blunt the\nfoe's fighting spirit.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_411: CYNDAQUIL protects itself by flaring up\nthe flames on its back.\nThe flames are vigorous if the POKéMON
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_412: is angry. However, if it is tired, the\nflames splutter fitfully with incomplete\ncombustion.
